rows.who: Wants to reproduce fak's determinism and benchmark results bit-for-bit, study the kernel from the research notes, and cite it.
rows.job: Reproduce the determinism witness and the benchmarks offline, read the research notes, and cite the work.

affordances.need: a committed bit-identical cross-arch determinism witness (the reproducibility proof)
a reproduce-offline runbook (the allow/deny boundary, deterministic)
the research notes / explainers a researcher reads to study the kernel
committed raw benchmark/results data (not just summary prose)
a benchmark quickstart / methodology so a result can be re-run
a citable DOI / archival deposit (Zenodo) so the work has a stable academic handle

gaps: CITATION.cff has no DOI yet (no Zenodo/archival deposit) — the citation is repo-URL only
Full native-753B serving is a multi-month track; most committed results are tiny-scale witnesses + GPU-kernel cosines

rows.who: Runs fak's benchmarks and compares the numbers against the SOTA field (vLLM / SGLang / TensorRT-LLM / llama.cpp).
rows.job: Run a benchmark command, read the measured numbers, and see fak's honest position vs the field.

affordances.need: a runnable benchmark command (the fan-out benchmark) that resolves to a real cmd dir
a benchmark-regression CI gate so the numbers don't silently rot
the industry scorecard (fak vs the SOTA field, with parity-debt)
the generated competitive standing doc with dated SOTA sources
a hardware bench plan / coverage matrix with exact go run invocations
a benchmark quickstart so a result is reproducible

gaps: Cross-machine GPU benchmarks need real hardware (GPU-server/GCP) — the laptop path is CPU-parity + tiny-scale witnesses

rows.who: An eng manager or tech lead deciding in ten minutes whether fak is real, honest, and worth a deeper look.
rows.job: See what fak is, what's proven, how it compares, and its license — fast, with no overclaim.

affordances.need: a quotable one-sentence identity near the top of the front page
an honesty ledger tagging every claim shipped/simulated/stub
an honest comparison vs the SOTA field
a durable/real/useful-today product map so a buyer can see what is shippable now
headline numbers traced to a commit + artifact (no naked claims)

gaps: No one-page pricing / TCO sheet — adoption cost is inferred from the deployment guide + the one-binary story
